nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical reef areas, coral rubble flats, and seagrass beds; typically takes shelter in crevices by day and emerges to graze at night.

decorPreferences: live rock with crevices for daytime shelter
mature aquascape with established coralline and film algae

diet.notes: Grazes continuously on film algae, filamentous and coralline algae, and diatoms across rock and glass. If algae stocks are thin, supplement with dried nori or algae wafers a few times per week; spine loss and reduced decorating behavior have been reported in underfed or stressed animals.

avoidWith: loose unglued coral frags (urchin may carry them off)

summary: Compact, reef-safe pincushion urchin with banded velvet test and short spines; generally considered one of the most popular ornamental urchins for nano and mid-size reef tanks thanks to its steady appetite for nuisance algae.
careNotes: Generally considered one of the easier marine urchins to keep, but is reported to be sensitive to copper-based medications and unstable water quality; loss of spines is commonly read as a sign of poor conditions or chronic underfeeding. Tends to wear shell fragments, rubble, snail shells, and small bits of decor on its test as camouflage, and has been known to pick up and carry unglued coral frags around the tank — secure frags with glue or putty if a Tuxedo Urchin is present. Color morphs sold in the trade as Blue Tuxedo (standard form, blue-black bands with dark spines), Red Tuxedo (red spines over blue/black bands), and Halloween Urchin (orange-and-black banded morph) are all the same species, Mespilia globulus, and have effectively identical husbandry. House in a tank that is at least a few months old so a steady film- and coralline-algae base is established; very young systems can starve them. A nano-reef floor of ~75 L is reasonable for a single specimen, with larger tanks needed if multiple animals are kept so each has enough grazing area.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Care sheet for Tuxedo Urchin / Blue Tuxedo Urchin (Mespilia globulus): max 3" (~7.6 cm), 20 gal minimum, 72-78°F, pH 8.1-8.4, dKH 8-12, SG 1.023-1.025, peaceful, reef-safe, herbivore; copper-sensitive.

additional.name: Reef2Reef Community
additional.notes: Designated secondary (Reef2Reef Wiki) returned 403 on direct fetch; substituted aggregated community threads (via WebSearch snippet) which confirm reef-safe status, ~3-4" captive size, hardiness of aquacultured specimens, and the frag-carrying behavior.

additional.name: Shrimp and Snail Breeder (AquariumBreeder) — Tuxedo Urchin Care Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Temnopleuridae, Camarodonta), water parameters (22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.021-1.025, dKH 8-12), 15-gal minimum, herbivore diet with nori supplementation, 1-3 year lifespan, and nocturnal grazing / decoration behavior. Used to average size and tank-volume guidance against the LiveAquaria figures.

additional.name: SeaLifeBase — Mespilia globulus
additional.notes: Used for taxonomic / native-range / depth verification (Indo-West Pacific, 0-60 m). Direct page returned 403 on fetch; data drawn from the SeaLifeBase summary surfaced via search.
